name: Umuganda Health
peopleReachedDefinition: Unique patients served across all clinic locations, deduplicated by patient registration number. While total visits are 24,500 annually, many patients make multiple visits — 9,800 represents unique individuals who received care.
householdsReachedDefinition: Estimated as unique patients (9,800) divided by average patients per household (1.5) = 6,533 households. Based on patient registry analysis cross-referenced with household health survey data.
jobsCreatedDefinition: Full-time equivalent (FTE) jobs directly created by Umuganda Health: 22 nurses and clinicians, 15 community health workers, 8 administrative and support staff. Verified through Ministry of Health registration and payroll.

outputs.label: Patient Visits Annually
outputs.fullName: Total Patient Visits Across All Clinic Locations
outputs.methodology: Cumulative patient visit count from electronic health records across all 8 clinic locations for the trailing 12-month period

outputs.label: Healthcare Workers Trained
outputs.fullName: Community Health Workers Trained and Certified
outputs.methodology: Count of community health workers who completed the 6-month training program and received Ministry of Health certification
outputs.source: Training program records and MoH certification database

outputs.label: Clinics Supported
outputs.fullName: Primary Care Clinics Built or Supported (IRIS+ OI1120)
outputs.methodology: Count of operational primary care clinics receiving Umuganda investment in infrastructure, equipment, or staffing

outputs.label: People Reached
outputs.fullName: Unique individual patients served across all clinic locations
outputs.methodology: Unique patient count after deduplication across all facilities. Each patient counted once regardless of number of visits.

outputs.label: Households Reached
outputs.fullName: Estimated unique households represented by patient population
outputs.methodology: Derived from unique patients divided by average patients per household (1.5), based on household health survey data
outputs.source: Patient intake records cross-referenced with household surveys

outputs.label: Jobs Created
outputs.fullName: Direct healthcare and support jobs created through Umuganda Health operations
outputs.methodology: Count of full-time equivalent clinical, community health, and administrative positions verified through payroll

outcomes.label: Maternal Mortality Reduction
outcomes.fullName: Reduction in Maternal Mortality Rate in Service Areas
outcomes.methodology: Comparison of maternal deaths per 100,000 live births in Umuganda catchment areas vs matched control districts without Umuganda facilities, using Rwanda HMIS data

outcomes.label: Cost vs Private Clinics
outcomes.fullName: Average Consultation Cost as Ratio of Private Sector
outcomes.methodology: Mystery shopper surveys comparing standard consultation fees at 5 comparable private facilities in each service area

outcomes.label: Patient Satisfaction
outcomes.methodology: Post-visit SMS surveys sent to patients 48 hours after visit, quarterly sample of n=200, NPS calculated as % Promoters minus % Detractors

outcomes.label: Women Beneficiary %
outcomes.fullName: Percentage of total patients who are female
outcomes.methodology: Calculated from sex-disaggregated patient registration data

financials.label: Revenue
financials.fullName: Semi-annual gross revenue from patient consultation fees, government health insurance reimbursements (CBHI/Mutuelle de Santé), and pharmaceutical dispensing revenue
financials.methodology: Gross revenue on accrual basis per IFRS 15. Includes patient consultation fees, government health insurance reimbursements (CBHI/Mutuelle de Santé), and pharmaceutical dispensing revenue.
financials.source: Audited financial statements and management accounts

financials.label: Valuation
financials.fullName: Fair market value of the fund's equity position
financials.methodology: Fair market value via DCF and comparable transactions.
financials.source: Independent valuation report and management estimate

financials.label: Capital Deployed
financials.fullName: Total committed capital called and deployed

profile.description: Umuganda Health operates a network of digitally-enabled primary healthcare clinics in underserved districts of Rwanda. The company integrates telemedicine, electronic health records, and community health worker networks to deliver affordable, quality primary care and maternal health services.
profile.theoryOfChange: By establishing accessible, affordable primary healthcare clinics equipped with digital health tools, Umuganda improves health outcomes through early detection and treatment of common diseases, reduces maternal mortality through antenatal care coverage, and decreases the burden on tertiary hospitals. Community health workers extend reach into rural areas, enabling preventive care and health education.
profile.targetBeneficiaries: Low-income populations in rural and peri-urban districts of Southern and Eastern Rwanda, with particular focus on pregnant women, children under 5, and elderly patients lacking access to quality primary care.
profile.geographicFocus: Southern Province (Huye, Nyanza, Gisagara) and Eastern Province (Ngoma, Kayonza). 12 operational clinics serving 45 sectors across 5 districts.
profile.implementationApproach: Hub-and-spoke model: 12 fixed clinics (hubs) supported by 120+ community health workers (spokes) who conduct household visits, referrals, and follow-ups. Telemedicine platform connects rural clinics with specialist physicians in Kigali.
profile.reportingFrequency: Semi-annual (H1/H2) impact assessments aligned with Rwanda Ministry of Health reporting cycles. Quarterly clinical quality audits by independent medical advisory board.
